name: GitLab MR Auto-Review & Risk Assessment
description: Automated workflow: GitLab MR Auto-Review & Risk Assessment. This workflow integrates 12 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, code, agent, merge. It contains 27 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: GitLab MR Auto-Review & Risk Assessment workflow containing 23 nodes.

nodes.name: Comment Back on MR

parameters.name: body
parameters.value: =# CodeSnape by Quantana **Summary** | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Summary }} --- | --- **Risk Level** | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.RiskLevel.replace(/<\/?b>/g, '') }} ## Recommendations | Recommendation | Code Snippet | | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Recommendations.map(rec => `| ${rec.Recommendation} | \`${rec.CodeSnippet}\` |`).join('\n') }} ## Test Cases | Test For | Steps | Expected Outcome | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.TestCases.map(test => `| ${test.TestFor} | ${test.Steps} | ${test.ExpectedOutcome} |`).join('\n') }} ## Issues | File | Potential Issue | Severity | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Issues.map(issue => `| ${issue.File} | ${issue.PotentialIssue} | ${issue.Severity} |`).join('\n') }} ## Diff Table | Line | Before | After | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.DiffTable.Changes.map(change => `| ${change.Line} | \`${change.Before}\` | \`${change.After}\` |`).join('\n') }}

nodes.notes: This httpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
- Triggers workflow when a merge request (MR) is created or updated.  

- Add GitLab credentials. Select merge_requests event.

nodes.notes: This gitlabTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
- Fetches code changes (diffs) from GitLab using API.  

- Replace Authorization token with your GitLab API key.

parameters.content:
- Ensures that MR contains changes before proceeding.

- Add Anthropic API Key (Claude AI).

- Sends an HTML-formatted MR Report to developers & QA teams.

- Add Gmail credentials.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
- Posts AI-generated review report as a GitLab MR comment.
- Replace Authorization token with your GitLab API key.

parameters.name: body
parameters.value: =# CodeSnape by Quantana **Summary** | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Summary }} --- | --- **Risk Level** | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.RiskLevel.replace(/<\/?b>/g, '') }} ## Recommendations | Recommendation | Code Snippet | | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Recommendations.map(rec => `| ${rec.Recommendation} | \`${rec.CodeSnippet}\` |`).join('\n') }} ## Test Cases | Test For | Steps | Expected Outcome | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.TestCases.map(test => `| ${test.TestFor} | ${test.Steps} | ${test.ExpectedOutcome} |`).join('\n') }} ## Issues | File | Potential Issue | Severity | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.Issues.map(issue => `| ${issue.File} | ${issue.PotentialIssue} | ${issue.Severity} |`).join('\n') }} ## Diff Table | Line | Before | After | | --- | --- | --- | {{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.DiffTable.Changes.map(change => `| ${change.Line} | \`${change.Before}\` | \`${change.After}\` |`).join('\n') }}

parameters.subject:
=[{{ $('AI Agent').item.json.output.RiskLevel.replace(/<\/?b>/g, '') }}] - CodeSnape for [{{ $('Merge').first().json.body.project.name }}] by [{{ $('Merge').first().json.body.user.name }}] at
{{ $('Merge').first().json.body.object_attributes.created_at }}

parameters.content:
- Triggers workflow when a merge request (MR) is created or updated.  

- Add GitLab credentials. Select merge_requests event.

- Fetches code changes (diffs) from GitLab using API.  

- Replace Authorization token with your GitLab API key.

- Ensures that MR contains changes before proceeding.

- No setup required.

- Calls Claude AI to analyze the diff and generate: Risk Level, Issues, Recommendations, Test Cases.

- Add Anthropic API Key (Claude AI).

- Cleans and refines AI output for structured reporting.
- No setup required.

- Cleans and refines AI output for structured reporting.
- No setup required.

- Creates a list of developers & QA testers for email notifications.

- Update email mappings for your team.

- Sends an HTML-formatted MR Report to developers & QA teams.

- Add Gmail credentials.

- Posts AI-generated review report as a GitLab MR comment.
- Replace Authorization token with your GitLab API key.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "GitLab Trigger".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "GitLab Trigger1".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "GitLab Trigger2".
Open the "Extract Diff" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If Some Change" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Auto-fixing Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Structured Output Parser" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Anthropic Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Anthropic Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Comment Back on MR" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send to DL ( Email Notification)" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
